ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Ti Yezu yu'urɛ to'e tinse'ehi en kaaɛ Galilii la za'a. Yezu tibɛ Pita dɛɛma la nɛreba basɛba (Matu
translated: And Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee, and a fame went out concerning him through all the region round about. And he taught in their synagogues, being glorified of all.

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Bala ti ba zom uureŋɔ yakɛ kiŋɛ muuen ha ba ma'a.
translated: They will come and take the child to the farm to show him the work.

ref: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
text: Bunsɛlenduma wa kampiihi buuri wa Yamam wan iŋɛ la ŋwani piiɛ ti ba kan gakɛ kɛ̃ ya ta bahɛ bugum tiŋa la puan? Dikɛ ya bahɛ bugum la puan?
translated: What will the students of the school in Yaman do when they see that the water has destroyed their farm and the Bugum town is also destroyed? What will happen to the Bugum town?

ref: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
text: gee yɛta ya mɛhi yeti, ‘Tumam yuum han boni, tu yaabe duma saŋa ha la, tumam yuum kan lagum ka'ɛ ni nɔtɔgeheba la ziim.’
translated: And they said to him, 'The Tumam people have come to our land, they have come to take our cattle, the Tumam people have come to destroy our farms and our homes.'

ref: When they saw him, they thought he was a ghost. They were terrified and started screaming.
text: Gee a zahereba la en nyɛ en ti a kini ko'om la zuo la, dabeem en kɛ̃' ba paaɛ paaɛ, ti ba yeti, “A dela kukɔ!” Gee kaahɛ la dabeem.
translated: They went to the farm and found that the yams were ready to be harvested, so they started digging carefully, and when they dug, they said, “The yams are ripe!” And they harvested the yams.
